billing.taxInfoBody: Each row shows the tax collected for one jurisdiction. **Jurisdiction**: regional code (e.g. US-CA, GB, DE). **Country**: ISO country code. **Rate**: applicable rate in the jurisdiction. **Collected**: total tax collected over the period.
billing.taxMockNotice: Demo data. Real Stripe Tax retrieval (Tax Transactions API) is planned for a future iteration.
billing.metricsInfoBody: **MRR**: monthly recurring revenue. **ARR**: MRR × 12. **Churn**: cancelled paid subs over 30d ÷ paid. **Conversion**: paid ÷ (paid + free). **ARPU**: MRR ÷ paid. **LTV**: ARPU ÷ churn (simple estimate).
billing.couponsInfoBody: On creation, a readable **promo code** is generated automatically (e.g. WELCOME20). Users enter it on the Pricing page at checkout. The Stripe coupon then applies the discount per the configured duration.
billing.dunningInfoBody: List of users with failed payments. **Step**: 0 = no issue, 1 = first failure, 2 = warning, 3 = scheduled downgrade. **Days Left**: days before automatic downgrade to FREE plan.

ai.sectionDescription: Choose the platform-wide default AI provider used by all users that have not configured a personal BYOK provider in their settings.
ai.defaultProviderHelp: Applies to every user without a custom AI provider. Use “auto” to fall back to priority-based selection (Gemini → OpenRouter → Ollama).
ai.loadError: Failed to load AI provider configuration.
ai.unreachableWarning: This provider is currently not reachable. Saving anyway will let users fall back to the platform default until it recovers.
ai.availabilityHelp: Live status of every registered provider. Unreachable ones will not be used by auto-selection.
ai.providerAutoDesc: Priority-based auto-selection: Gemini → OpenRouter → Ollama → first available.
ai.providerGeminiDesc: Google Gemini API. Fast, affordable, multimodal.
ai.providerOpenRouterDesc: OpenRouter — unified access to 200+ models, including free options.
ai.providerOllamaDesc: Self-hosted Ollama. No data leaves your infrastructure.
ai.providerAnthropicDesc: Anthropic Claude. Excellent reasoning and long context.
ai.customSectionHelp: Plug your own AI backend (Gemini, Claude, OpenAI, LM Studio, llama.cpp, Ollama, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint). Each entry becomes a selectable default for every user without BYOK.
ai.noCustomProviders: No custom providers yet. Add one to plug your own AI backend.
ai.testMismatch: Reachable, but the configured model "{{configured}}" is not loaded. Available: {{models}}
ai.localServerNote1: Local servers (LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM) may ignore client-supplied temperature, max-tokens or model selection. Set them in the provider UI as well.
ai.localServerNote2: Most local models do not support OpenAI tool-calling, so the AI Coach falls back to context-stuffed prompts (limited to a recent slice of trades).
ai.localServerNote3: Use "Test" after saving to list models actually loaded on the server and confirm the identifier above matches.
ai.deleteDesc: Users currently using this provider will fall back to the auto default. The configuration cannot be recovered.
ai.dialogDesc: Configure a custom AI backend that becomes selectable as the platform default.
ai.fieldNameHelp: Lowercase, alphanumeric, dash or underscore. Used as the default-provider key.
ai.baseUrlHelpOpenAi: OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Examples: LM Studio (http://host:1234/v1), llama.cpp server, vLLM, Azure OpenAI.
ai.baseUrlHelpGeneric: Optional override. Leave blank to use the provider default.
ai.apiKeyHelp: Stored AES-256-GCM encrypted. Only required for cloud providers (Gemini, Anthropic, OpenRouter).
ai.apiKeyEditHelp: Leave blank to keep the existing key. Provide a new value to rotate it.
ai.timeoutHelp: Bump for slow local reasoning models (qwen3, deepseek-r1, etc.). Default is 300s. Leave blank to use the default.

US.headline: US Capital Gains Tax — IRS Rules
US.rate1: Short-term (held ≤ 1 year): taxed as ordinary income — 10% to 37%
US.rate3: Additional 3.8% Net Investment Income Tax (NIIT) if MAGI > $200K/$250K
US.rate5: Section 475(f) MTM election available for qualifying traders
US.lossCarryforward: Unlimited capital loss carryforward; up to $3,000 deductible against ordinary income per year
US.warning1: Wash Sale Rule: losses on securities repurchased within 30 days are disallowed. The disallowed loss is added to the cost basis of the replacement security.
US.note1: Crypto assets are treated as property — same capital gains rules apply
US.note2: Broker-issued Form 1099-B must reconcile with your Form 8949

FR.headline: French Tax Rules — PFU and Progressive Scale
FR.rate1: PFU (Flat Tax): 30% — 12.8% income tax + 17.2% social contributions (CSG/CRDS)
FR.rate3: Choose the most favorable option when filing (box 2OP)
FR.form2: 2074 — Capital gains and miscellaneous gains (if sales > €50,000)
FR.deadline: May / June depending on zone (zone 1: ~May 22, zone 2: ~May 29, zone 3: ~June 5)
FR.lossCarryforward: 10 years for capital losses on securities
FR.warning1: 3916-bis declaration MANDATORY for any account with a foreign broker. Fine: €1,500 per undeclared account per year (€10,000 for non-cooperative countries).
FR.warning2: The PFU applies automatically. The progressive scale option is global and irrevocable for the year.
FR.note1: Capital gains are calculated in euros at the date of sale — foreign currency gains are converted
FR.note2: Cryptocurrencies are taxed under the digital assets regime (art. 150 VH bis CGI)
FR.note3: CFDs and forex are treated as financial products — same PFU rules apply

CA.headline: Canada — CRA Capital Gains Rules
CA.rate1: Investor (capital gains): 50% inclusion rate — only half of gains are taxable
CA.rate2: Day trader / business income: 100% inclusion — full gains taxable as income
CA.rate4: Capital gains inclusion rate: verify current status with CRA
CA.form2: T2125 — Statement of Business or Professional Activities — for traders
CA.deadline: April 30 (self-employed: June 15, but tax owed still due April 30)
CA.lossCarryforward: Capital losses: carry back 3 years, carry forward indefinitely
CA.warning1: Superficial Loss Rule: if you sell at a loss and repurchase the same security within 30 days, the loss is denied.
CA.warning2: TFSA Warning: day-trading inside a TFSA can cause the CRA to reclassify gains as business income — fully taxable.
CA.note1: CRA classifies traders vs investors based on: frequency, holding period, financing, time spent, and knowledge
CA.note2: Foreign currency gains/losses are also taxable in CAD terms
CA.note3: ACB (Adjusted Cost Base) must be tracked per security across all accounts

MA.headline: Morocco — Tax on Capital Gains (CGI)
MA.rate3: Exemption if total sales are below 30,000 MAD/year
MA.rate4: NEW 2026: Tax payment within 30 days of each sale for foreign broker accounts
MA.form1: Annual IR declaration (DGI form) — before April 1
MA.deadline: 30 days per transaction (foreign brokers) + annual declaration before April 1
MA.warning1: NEW 2026: For accounts with foreign brokers (Exness, XM, etc.), each sale must be declared and tax paid within 30 days. Do not wait for the annual declaration.
MA.warning2: Office des Changes: fund transfers abroad for investment are limited to 20,000 MAD/year for individuals. Forex/CFD trading remains in a regulatory grey area.
MA.note1: Capital gains on shares listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange benefit from a 5-year exemption at IPO
MA.note2: Capital gains in foreign currencies must be converted to MAD at the exchange rate on the date of sale
MA.note3: UCITS equities benefit from the same treatment as listed shares (15%)
MA.note4: Voluntary regularization is possible before a tax audit with a reduced surcharge

UK.headline: UK Capital Gains Tax — HMRC Rules
UK.rate2: Basic rate taxpayer: 10% on gains (18% on residential property)
UK.form2: CGT real-time service for one-off disposals
UK.deadline: January 31 (Self Assessment) or 60 days for UK residential property
UK.lossCarryforward: Capital losses carry forward indefinitely; must be claimed within 4 years
UK.warning1: Bed and breakfast rule: selling and rebuying the same shares within 30 days means the repurchased shares' cost is used.
UK.note1: Section 104 pool: shares of the same class are averaged into one pool for CGT purposes

DE.headline: Germany — Abgeltungsteuer (Flat Rate Withholding Tax)
DE.deadline: July 31 (tax year, with extension to February 28 of the following year)
DE.lossCarryforward: Loss offsetting only with capital gains; unlimited loss carryforward
DE.warning1: Losses from derivatives (CFD, futures) may only be offset against gains from derivatives since 2021 — capped at €20,000/year.
DE.note1: CFD losses are subject to a loss offsetting restriction since 2021 (§ 20 para. 6 EStG)

AU.headline: Australia — ATO Capital Gains Tax
AU.rate1: CGT discount: 50% reduction for assets held > 12 months (individuals)
AU.rate2: Net capital gain included in assessable income — taxed at marginal rates
AU.deadline: October 31 (or May 15 if using a tax agent)
AU.lossCarryforward: Capital losses carried forward indefinitely; cannot offset against income
AU.warning1: Trader vs investor: the ATO may classify frequent traders as carrying on a business — gains fully assessable, losses immediately deductible.
AU.note1: Crypto is taxed as CGT asset — same 50% discount applies if held > 12 months

OTHER.headline: Generic Tax Summary
OTHER.rate1: Tax rates vary by jurisdiction — consult a local tax professional
OTHER.form1: Varies by country — export the CSV summary and provide it to your tax advisor
OTHER.note1: This tool provides a generic capital gains summary. Always verify with a qualified tax professional.

landing.faqFreeQ: Is the Free plan really free?
landing.faqFreeA: Yes! The Free plan includes 1 broker connection, up to 100 trades, basic analytics, and a 30-day market history. No credit card required.
landing.faqTrialQ: Do paid plans have a free trial?
landing.faqTrialA: Yes, all paid plans come with a 14-day free trial. You can cancel anytime during the trial period without being charged.
landing.faqBrokersA: We support MetaTrader 5 (MT5), Interactive Brokers (IBKR), cTrader, and manual trade entry. More brokers are being added regularly.
landing.faqDataA: Absolutely. All data is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3). Broker credentials are encrypted with AES-256-GCM and we never store plaintext passwords.
landing.faqCancelQ: Can I cancel or change my plan anytime?
landing.faqCancelA: Yes, you can upgrade, downgrade, or cancel at any time from your Account Management page. Changes take effect at the end of your current billing period.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.s1Content: FollowUp Trading is committed to protecting the privacy of its users. This privacy policy describes how we collect, use, store, and protect your personal information when you use our trading journal platform.
privacy.s2Content: We collect the following types of data:
s2List: Account information: email address, username, password (hashed)
Trading data: trade history imported from your connected brokers (positions, P&L, symbols, dates)
Broker credentials: login credentials for your brokerage accounts (encrypted with AES-256-GCM)
Usage data: activity logs, interface preferences, notification settings
Technical data: IP address, browser type, device fingerprint (for security purposes only)
s3List: Provide and improve trading journal services
Calculate your performance metrics and statistics
Sync your trades from your brokerage accounts
Ensure account security (detection of suspicious logins, lockout after failed attempts)
Send alerts and notifications you have configured
privacy.s4Content: We take the security of your data very seriously:
s4List: Broker credentials are encrypted with AES-256-GCM with key rotation
Authentication uses short-lived JWT tokens (15 minutes)
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is available via TOTP and email
Accounts are locked after 5 failed login attempts
privacy.s5Content: We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for commercial purposes. Your trading data is never shared without your explicit consent. The only third-party communications involve your broker APIs for trade synchronization.
privacy.s6Content: Your data is retained as long as your account is active. You may request complete deletion of your account and all associated data at any time. Deleted data is purged from our systems within 30 days.
privacy.s7Content: In accordance with GDPR, you have the following rights:
s7List: Access: request a copy of your personal data
Deletion: request deletion of your data
Portability: export your data in a standard format (CSV, PDF)
Objection: object to the processing of your data
privacy.s8Content: We only use essential cookies required for the application to function (authentication, interface preferences). We do not use advertising tracking cookies or third-party cookies for marketing purposes.
privacy.s9Content: For any questions regarding this privacy policy or to exercise your rights, please contact us via our contact page.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.s1Content: By accessing FollowUp Trading and using our services, you agree to be bound by these terms of service. If you do not accept these terms, please do not use our platform.
terms.s2Content: FollowUp Trading is a trading journal platform that allows users to:
s2List: Import and track trades from multiple brokers
Analyze trading performance via detailed metrics and statistics
Maintain a trading journal with notes and annotations
Receive alerts and insights based on trading data
terms.s3Content: As a user, you agree to:
s3List: Maintain the confidentiality of your login credentials
Not use the platform for illegal activities
Respect applicable laws regarding financial trading
Not attempt to circumvent security measures
terms.s4Content: FollowUp Trading is a tracking and analysis tool, not a financial advisory service. We do not provide investment advice, trading signals, or financial recommendations. Trading involves risk and past performance does not guarantee future results. We are not responsible for financial losses resulting from trading decisions.
terms.s5Content: All content, features, and functionality of FollowUp Trading are owned by us and are protected by copyright, trademark, and other intellectual property laws. You may not reproduce, distribute, or modify any part of the platform without prior written authorization.
terms.s6Content: Paid subscriptions are billed monthly or annually as selected. You can upgrade, downgrade, or cancel at any time. Changes take effect at the end of the current billing period. Refunds are handled on a case-by-case basis.
terms.s7Content: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account in case of violation of these terms. You may delete your account at any time, which will result in the permanent deletion of all your data within 30 days.
terms.s8Content: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Users will be notified of significant changes via email or in-app notification. Continued use after modification constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.s1Content: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the site function properly and provide a better user experience.
cookies.s2Content: We only use cookies that are strictly necessary for the platform to function:
s2List: Authentication token: keeps you logged in during your session
Theme preference: remembers your light/dark mode choice
Language preference: stores your chosen language (EN, FR, ES)
Sidebar state: remembers collapsed/expanded navigation sections
cookies.s3Content: We do not use any of the following:
cookies.s4Content: You can control cookies through your browser settings. However, disabling essential cookies may prevent the platform from functioning correctly. Since we only use essential cookies, there is no cookie consent banner — all cookies are strictly necessary for the service.
cookies.s5Content: We may update this cookie policy from time to time. Any changes will be reflected on this page with an updated date.

riskMetrics.title: Risk Metrics
riskMetrics.description: In-depth risk analysis of your trading portfolio
riskMetrics.errorDescription: There was an error loading your risk metrics. Please try again later.

overview.riskProfileInfo: A radar view of your key risk metrics normalized to a 0-100 scale. Higher values are generally better (except VaR where lower is better). Helps you see strengths and weaknesses at a glance.
overview.keyRiskMetricsInfo: Core risk and performance ratios computed from your actual closed trades. These are the numbers professional traders and fund managers use to evaluate performance quality.
overview.riskSummaryInfo: Quick snapshot of your risk distribution metrics from closed trade history. Standard deviation measures overall volatility, downside deviation only measures negative volatility.
overview.stdDeviationInfo: Standard deviation of your trade returns. Measures overall volatility of your trading results. Lower means more predictable outcomes.
overview.downsideDevInfo: Downside deviation: volatility of only the negative returns. Used in Sortino ratio. Lower is better as it means smaller losses.
overview.timeInMarketInfo: Percentage of time you had open positions during the analysis period. Shows how actively you trade vs staying on the sidelines.
overview.diversityScoreInfo: Portfolio diversification score (0-100) based on the Herfindahl-Hirschman Index. Higher means your exposure is spread across more symbols. Low score means concentrated risk.

metrics.sharpeRatioInfo: Measures risk-adjusted return. Compares your excess return (above risk-free rate) to its volatility. Above 1.0 is good, above 2.0 is excellent.
metrics.sortinoRatioInfo: Like Sharpe but only penalizes downside volatility (losses). A higher Sortino means your losses are small relative to your gains.
metrics.maxDrawdownInfo: The largest peak-to-trough decline in your equity curve. Shows the worst loss you experienced from a high point before recovering.
metrics.currentDrawdownInfo: How far your current equity is below the all-time peak. 0% means you are at or above your previous high.
metrics.recoveryFactorInfo: Total profits divided by maximum drawdown. Shows how efficiently you recover from losses. Higher is better.
metrics.profitConsistencyInfo: Score (0-100) measuring how consistently you profit each week. Combines win rate, stability of returns, and trend persistence.

var.var95Info: Value at Risk at 95% confidence: the maximum loss expected in 95% of trading outcomes. In 1 out of 20 trades, you may lose more than this amount.
var.var99Info: Value at Risk at 99% confidence: the maximum loss expected in 99% of trading outcomes. A more conservative estimate — only 1% of trades may exceed this loss.
var.cvar95Info: Conditional Value at Risk: the average loss in the worst 5% of trades. Unlike VaR, this tells you HOW BAD things get when they go wrong.
var.pnlDistributionInfo: Histogram showing how your trade P&L values are distributed across ranges. A bell-shaped curve centered above zero indicates consistent profitability. Heavy left tail means occasional large losses.
var.noDistribution: No P&L distribution data for this period
var.varFootnote: Value at Risk (VaR) represents the maximum potential loss at a given confidence level over a defined period. A lower VaR relative to your equity indicates better risk management.

stress.title: Stress Test Scenarios
stress.info: These scenarios are derived from YOUR actual trading data, not generic market events. They show what has happened and what could happen based on your real P&L patterns. Use them to check if your position sizing can survive worst-case outcomes.
stress.description: Impact analysis based on your actual trading history and hypothetical stress scenarios
stress.noData: No trade data available for stress analysis
stress.worstSingleTradeDesc: Your largest single-trade loss from actual trading history.
stress.worstSingleTradeInfo: This is the biggest loss you have experienced on a single closed trade. It represents your tail risk per position.
stress.maxDrawdownInfo: Maximum cumulative loss from a peak before recovery. This is the worst sustained losing streak in your equity curve.
stress.avgLoss3xDesc: Hypothetical: a single trade losing 3x your average loss.
stress.avgLoss3xInfo: A stress scenario assuming one trade loses 3 times your average losing trade amount. Tests resilience against an outsized loss.
stress.fiveLossesDesc: Hypothetical: five consecutive trades hitting your average loss.
stress.fiveLossesInfo: A streak scenario: 5 losing trades in a row at your average loss size. Tests if your account can survive a bad streak.
stress.tenPctDrawdownInfo: A benchmark scenario showing what a 10% portfolio drawdown looks like in dollar terms given your current equity.

direction.title: Long vs Short Performance
direction.info: Compares your performance on long (buy) trades vs short (sell) trades. Helps identify if you have a directional bias or if one direction is significantly more profitable than the other.
direction.description: Breakdown of your trading performance by direction
direction.noData: No directional trade data for this period
direction.longInfo: Trades where you bought first expecting the price to go up. Shows total P&L, number of trades, and win rate for all your long positions.
direction.shortInfo: Trades where you sold first expecting the price to go down. Shows total P&L, number of trades, and win rate for all your short positions.
direction.comparisonInfo: Visual comparison of P&L and trade counts between your long and short trades. Helps identify which direction contributes more to your profitability.

allocation.assetAllocationInfo: Shows the distribution of your trading activity across different asset types (Forex, Stocks, Crypto, etc.) based on P&L contribution. Larger slices mean more P&L impact from that asset class.
allocation.pnlByAssetInfo: Detailed breakdown showing the profit or loss contribution of each asset type. Green means profitable, red means losing. The percentage shows relative weight in your total trading activity.
allocation.noData: No asset type data for this period
allocation.diversificationScoreInfo: Herfindahl-Hirschman based score (0-100). Higher means better diversification across symbols. A score below 40 means concentrated risk in few positions.
allocation.diversificationLow: Low diversification. Consider trading more asset types to reduce concentrated risk.
allocation.diversificationMedium: Moderate diversification. Adding exposure to non-correlated assets would improve risk.

margin.title: Margin Utilization
margin.info: Tracks how much of your available margin is being used. Staying below 60% is considered safe. Exceeding 80% increases the risk of margin calls.

holding.title: Holding Period Analysis
holding.info: Analyzes how trade duration affects your performance. Helps identify your optimal holding time — whether you perform better on scalps, intraday, or swing trades.
holding.noData: No closed trades in this range.

kelly.title: Kelly Criterion per Strategy
kelly.info: Kelly formula computes optimal position sizing from each strategy's historical win rate and avg win/loss. 'Recommended' is half-Kelly (the conservative practitioner's cut). 'Aggressive' is 0.7 × Kelly. Never bet full Kelly in practice.
kelly.description: Based on your closed trades — strategies with fewer trades have less reliable Kelly estimates.
kelly.noData: No closed trades tagged with a strategy in this range.
kelly.fullKellyTitle: Full Kelly (do not use in practice)
